# Awesome Architecture 2.0 [![Awesome](https://awesome.re/badge.svg)](https://awesome.re)

<!-- Generated by tools/render_awesome.py from tools/registry/*.yml. -->

A curated list of tools, simulators, proxies, datasets, benchmarks, and
workflows that support auditable AI-assisted computing-system design loops.
The public registry at [arch2.mlsysbook.ai/tools](https://arch2.mlsysbook.ai/tools/)
shows the same source records with availability and link-verification status.

## How to Contribute

Use the [tool submission form](https://github.com/harvard-edge/arch2/issues/new?template=submit_tool.yml)
to propose an entry for maintainer review.

## Contents

- [Simulation and Execution Environments](#simulation-and-execution-environments)
- [Proxy Models and Surrogate Predictors](#proxy-models-and-surrogate-predictors)
- [Agentic Workflows and Generators](#agentic-workflows-and-generators)
- [Data Representations and Hardware Vocabularies](#data-representations-and-hardware-vocabularies)
- [Verification and Red-Teaming](#verification-and-red-teaming)
- [Benchmarks and Datasets](#benchmarks-and-datasets)
- [Physical Design and EDA](#physical-design-and-eda)

## Simulation and Execution Environments

| Tool or artifact | Availability | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **[gem5](https://www.gem5.org/)** | Source available; link checked 2026-07-10 | Modular computer-system simulator for architecture feedback needing workload execution and reproducible state. |
| **[FireSim](https://fires.im/)** | Source available; link checked 2026-07-10 | FPGA-accelerated full-system simulation for when the loop needs stronger hardware/software feedback than a proxy can provide. |
| **[Chipyard](https://chipyard.readthedocs.io/)** | Source available; link checked 2026-07-10 | Integrated framework for generating and evaluating hardware systems, connecting generators, RTL, and simulation. |
| **[SST](https://sst-simulator.org/)** | Source available; link checked 2026-07-10 | Modular parallel simulation framework for exploring systems where ISA, microarchitecture, memory, programming model, and communication interact. |
| **[ChampSim](https://github.com/ChampSim/ChampSim)** | Source available; link checked 2026-07-10 | Trace-based simulator for fast feedback on cache, branch prediction, prefetching, and memory-hierarchy ideas. |
| **[GPGPU-Sim](https://github.com/gpgpu-sim/gpgpu-sim_distribution)** | Source available; link checked 2026-07-10 | Detailed GPU simulator for CUDA and OpenCL workloads when a loop needs microarchitectural feedback for GPU designs. |
| **[Sniper](https://snipersim.org/)** | Source available; link checked 2026-07-10 | Fast multicore x86 simulator based on interval core modeling for many-core architecture tradeoff studies. |
| **[zsim](https://github.com/s5z/zsim)** | Source available; link checked 2026-07-10 | Fast x86-64 multicore simulator focused on memory hierarchies and large heterogeneous systems. |
| **[Ramulator 2.0](https://github.com/CMU-SAFARI/ramulator2)** | Source available; link checked 2026-07-10 | Modular cycle-accurate DRAM simulator for evaluating memory-system designs, DRAM standards, and mitigation ideas. |
| **[DRAMsim3](https://github.com/umd-memsys/DRAMsim3)** | Source available; link checked 2026-07-10 | Cycle-accurate, thermal-capable DRAM simulator supporting DDR, LPDDR, GDDR, HBM, HMC, and related protocols. |
| **[BookSim](https://github.com/booksim/booksim2)** | Source available; link checked 2026-07-10 | Cycle-accurate interconnection-network simulator for studying NoC topologies, routing, and router microarchitecture. |
| **[SCALE-Sim](https://github.com/scalesim-project/SCALE-Sim)** | Source available; link checked 2026-07-10 | Systolic-array accelerator simulator for quickly exploring DNN accelerator design spaces. |
| **[Verilator](https://www.veripool.org/verilator/)** | Source available; link checked 2026-07-10 | The fastest free Verilog HDL simulator, converting Verilog to C++/SystemC. |

## Proxy Models and Surrogate Predictors

| Tool or artifact | Availability | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **[Apollo](https://arxiv.org/abs/2102.01723)** | Paper only; link checked 2026-07-10 | An automated framework for fast, accurate, and transferable architecture design space exploration using surrogate models. |
| **[Aladdin](https://github.com/harvard-acc/ALADDIN)** | Source available; link checked 2026-07-10 | Pre-RTL power and performance simulator for accelerator-centric design-space exploration. |
| **[MAESTRO](https://github.com/maestro-project/maestro)** | Source available; link checked 2026-07-10 | Analytical cost model for DNN dataflows and tiling; a fast-feedback model for dataflow exploration. |
| **[Accelergy](https://github.com/Accelergy-Project/accelergy)** | Source available; link checked 2026-07-10 | Energy-estimation infrastructure for accelerators, providing an explicit energy feedback source. |
| **[Timeloop](https://github.com/NVlabs/timeloop)** | Source available; link checked 2026-07-10 | Mapping and modeling tool for tensor workloads on accelerator architectures. |
| **[McPAT](https://github.com/HewlettPackard/mcpat)** | Source available; link checked 2026-07-10 | Power, area, and timing modeling framework for multicore and manycore architectures. |
| **[CACTI](https://github.com/HewlettPackard/cacti)** | Source available; link checked 2026-07-10 | Analytical model for cache and memory access time, cycle time, area, and power. |

## Agentic Workflows and Generators

| Tool or artifact | Availability | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **[ArchGym](https://github.com/srivatsankrishnan/oss-arch-gym)** | Source available; link checked 2026-07-10 | Open-source gym environment for evaluating machine learning algorithms in computer architecture exploration. |
| **[AutoChip](https://github.com/shailja-thakur/AutoChip)** | Source available; link checked 2026-07-10 | Conversational hardware design using LLMs to generate Verilog from natural language specifications. |
| **[ChipNeMo](https://arxiv.org/abs/2311.00176)** | Paper only; link checked 2026-07-10 | Domain-adapted LLMs for chip design, demonstrating automated EDA script generation and bug analysis. |
| **[VeriGen](https://arxiv.org/abs/2308.00708)** | Paper only; link checked 2026-07-10 | A large language model customized for generating functional Verilog code. |

## Data Representations and Hardware Vocabularies

| Tool or artifact | Availability | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **[Chisel](https://www.chisel-lang.org/)** | Source available; link checked 2026-07-10 | A hardware construction language embedded in Scala that provides high-level primitives for RTL generation. |
| **[PyMTL3](https://github.com/pymtl/pymtl3)** | Source available; link checked 2026-07-10 | An open-source Python-based hardware generation and simulation framework. |
| **[FIRRTL](https://github.com/chipsalliance/firrtl)** | Source available; link checked 2026-07-10 | A flexible intermediate representation for RTL, serving as the bridge between high-level generators and EDA tools. |

## Verification and Red-Teaming

| Tool or artifact | Availability | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **[CocoTB](https://github.com/cocotb/cocotb)** | Source available; link checked 2026-07-10 | A coroutine-based cosimulation library for writing VHDL and Verilog testbenches in Python. |
| **[SymbiYosys](https://symbiyosys.readthedocs.io/en/latest/)** | Source available; link checked 2026-07-10 | Front-end for Yosys-based formal verification flows, allowing agents to assert mathematical proofs against their generated RTL. |

## Benchmarks and Datasets

| Tool or artifact | Availability | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **[CVDP Benchmark](https://github.com/NVlabs/cvdp_benchmark)** | Benchmark available; link checked 2026-07-10 | Comprehensive Verilog Design Problems for RTL design and verification. Crucial for loops involving HDL generation and test harnesses. |
| **[RTLLM](https://github.com/hkust-zhiyao/RTLLM)** | Benchmark available; link checked 2026-07-10 | Benchmark for natural-language-to-RTL generation with Verilog designs, descriptions, and testbenches. |
| **[RTL-Repo](https://github.com/AUCOHL/RTL-Repo)** | Dataset available; link checked 2026-07-10 | Benchmark for evaluating LLMs on Verilog code completion within larger RTL repositories. |
| **[MLPerf Inference](https://github.com/mlcommons/inference)** | Benchmark available; link checked 2026-07-10 | MLCommons benchmark suite for measuring how fast systems run trained models across deployment scenarios. |
| **[QuArch](https://quarch.ai/)** | Benchmark available; link checked 2026-07-10 | Architecture question-answering and reasoning benchmark to test if a model can reason over architecture concepts. |
| **[VerilogEval](https://github.com/NVlabs/verilog-eval)** | Benchmark available; link checked 2026-07-10 | Specification-to-RTL and Verilog code-generation benchmark with executable checks. |
| **[KernelBench](https://github.com/ScalingIntelligence/KernelBench)** | Benchmark available; link checked 2026-07-10 | GPU-kernel generation benchmark with correctness and performance evaluation. |
| **[CircuitNet](https://github.com/circuitnet/CircuitNet)** | Dataset available; link checked 2026-07-10 | VLSI CAD dataset for machine-learning applications in EDA. |

## Physical Design and EDA

| Tool or artifact | Availability | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **[OpenROAD](https://theopenroadproject.org/)** | Source available; link checked 2026-07-10 | Open-source RTL-to-GDS flow for loops that need physical-design feedback and evidence. |
| **[ChiPBench](https://openreview.net/forum?id=gDkQ5iesrI)** | Paper only; link checked 2026-07-10 | Benchmark focused on end-to-end physical-design impact for AI chip placement. |
